stem: An engineer takes 25 measurements of fuel level using a fuel gauge. The measurements have a mean of 31.6 % and a standard deviation of 3.6 %. However, calibration tests reveal that a fuel gauge systematically underestimates the true value by between 1.5% and 7.5%. Estimate the true fuel level and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 31.6 %. After correcting for the 4.5% bias: ≈ 33.1 %. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (1.5%-7.5%) and sampling variability: [30.7, 35.6]. Point estimate: 33.1 %.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
